items.title: Winnipeg and surrounding area drenched in wild night of stormy weather
items.summary_auto: Southern Manitoba saw a wild night of storms with monsoon-like downpours, unconfirmed reports of tornadoes and vehicles abandoned on flooded roads.

items.title: 'Always wash your fruits': Calgary family finds black widow in grapes
items.summary_auto: A Calgary family will never look at grapes the same way again after finding a highly venomous black widow in their imported produce — an occurrence one entomologist says is becoming increasingly common in Canada.

items.title: Manitoba sought U.S. help to develop large AI data centre and find private financial partner on $18B hydro dam
items.summary_auto: Manitoba's government spoke last fall with U.S. energy advisers about developing a hyperscale AI data centre near Winnipeg and using private capital to build an $18-billion hydroelectric generating station on the Nelson River, documents obtained by CBC News show.

items.title: Public libraries relying more on fundraising to balance books
items.summary_auto: As provincial funding stagnates, libraries are turning to other sources to make up budget shortfalls and avoid branch closures.

items.title: 'Feels like harassment': Montreal café owner says years of language inspections taking a toll
items.summary_auto: The owner of Montreal's SoLIT Café says she has spent years making changes requested by Quebec’s language watchdog but still worries there is no clear end to the process. The OQLF says inspections stem from complaints made by the public.

items.title: CTE ruins lives but can’t be confirmed until you’re dead. Canadian scientists are trying to change that
items.summary_auto: Former soldier Brendan Hynes thinks he has CTE, but the devastating brain disease can’t be confirmed until after someone’s dead. Could cutting-edge Canadian research be the solution?

items.title: Charged with fraud, Manitoba man still selling shipping container pools under new business name
items.summary_auto: Once featured on HGTV, Manitoba pool builder Kurt Wittin now faces 26 counts of fraud with his past businesses, but still continues to sell shipping container pools under a new company and name.

items.title: Canadaland publisher apologizes in court to mother of WE Charity co-founders
items.summary_auto: The editor and publisher of Canadaland agreed to apologize to the mother of the WE Charity's co-founders as part of a settlement in a defamation lawsuit.

items.title: Ex-member of top climate body says Alberta pipeline deal is not compatible with net-zero
items.summary_auto: A former member of Canada's top climate body said the pipeline deal Canada signed with Alberta is incompatible with Carney government's net-zero target.

items.title: What does the Texas livestock ban mean for Alberta rodeos?
items.summary_auto: Livestock that have been in Texas within the past 21 days won't be allowed to enter Canada due to screwworms, but Lone Star State rodeo athletes aren’t willing to let the flesh-eating parasite stop them from competing in Canadian events — including the Calgary Stampede, one of the most lucrative rodeos in the world.

items.title: Setting a foundation: What we've learned about the PWHL's 4 newest expansion teams
items.summary_auto: New PWHL teams in Hamilton, Detroit, Las Vegas and San Jose each signed five players as foundational players this week. The expansion teams will begin play next season.

items.title: 2 First Nations in B.C. withdraw legal challenge of Ksi Lisims LNG project
items.summary_auto: The news come as Ksi Lisims recently announced preliminary supply deals with German utilities, and the partners aim to make a final go-ahead decision this year.

items.title: Feds extend amnesty period for firearms ban pending Supreme Court ruling
items.summary_auto: The Liberal government is extending the amnesty period for gun owners to comply with its "assault-style" firearm ban pending a Supreme Court ruling on the policy.

items.title: Person in custody after Ontario Provincial Police officer killed in line of duty near Hearst, Ont.
items.summary_auto: The Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) have arrested an 18-year-old man for first-degree murder in the death of an OPP constable near Hearst, Ont.

items.title: Would-be drivers in B.C. can now take learner's licence knowledge test online and at home
items.summary_auto: Would-be drivers in British Columbia are now being allowed to take the knowledge test for their learner's licence online and at home.

items.title: Canada's top justice says tight resources are delaying trials, harming judges' health
items.summary_auto: Underfunding of Canada's judicial system is causing trials to drag on past their legal limits, significantly boosting the number of unrepresented people and worsening mental and physical health of the country's judges, says the country's highest-ranking justice.

items.title: Sask. murder trial hears audio from night of 30-year-old mother's death, recorded by hockey mom in next room
items.summary_auto: As a lawyer prepared to play an audio recording that captured some of the last minutes of his daughter's life, Darris Dobersheck and his wife Hazel got up and quietly left the courtroom.
